channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: World Cup: Argentina face FIFA sanction over Falklands banner display
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Argentina could face FIFA sanction after players displayed a Falklands banner following their World Cup semi-final win. Learn more about the political mess
Read More: https://punchng.com/world-cup-argentina-face-fifa-sanction-over-falklands-banner-display/

item.title: Tuchel defends tactics after England World Cup semi final loss
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
England manager Thomas Tuchel defends his tactics after the Three Lions’ World Cup semi-final defeat to Argentina, despite facing scrutiny for his decision
Read More: https://punchng.com/tuchel-defends-tactics-after-england-world-cup-semi-final-loss/

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The Sky Sports Racing cameras head to Chepstow and Ffos Las, with all the action live from 2.05pm.

item.title: How TETFund’s Maiden Research Fair Is Setting The Stage For Bigger Innovation Revolution
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The maiden National Research Fair and Exhibition organised by the Tertiary Education Trust Fund (TETFund) in 2024 has opened a new chapter in Nigeria’s quest to transform research innovations into products, services and solutions that can drive economic growth. Building on the success of the first e

item.title: World Cup: I’m gutted – Harry Kane explains why England lost 2-1 to Argentina
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: England captain, Harry Kane, has explained why his team lost 2-1 against Argentina in the World Cup semi-final on Wednesday. Kane, who admitted that he was gutted by the outcome of the match, said Thomas Tuchel’s side didn’t do enough after going 1-0 up in the 55th minute of the enc

item.title: Tuchel retains FA backing despite England defeat
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Thomas Tuchel retains the backing of the Football Association despite England failing to reach the World Cup final.

item.title: Troops intercept suspected Boko Haram logistics suppliers, recover food, medicines in Borno
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have intercepted suspected Boko Haram/Islamic State West Africa Province (ISWAP) logistics suppliers and recovered food items, veterinary medicines and other supplies during an operation in Damboa Local Government Area of Borno State. The development was disclosed in a

item.title: Flood crisis: Sanwo-Olu orders overhaul of Lekki development plans
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Lagos Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu, on Wednesday, ordered a review of the master plans of flood-prone communities, particularly within the Lekki corridor, as part of fresh measures to address persistent flooding and restore natural drainage systems. The governor announced the decision during an insp

item.title: Alleged sexual assault: NANS demands justice over UNIZIK lecturer scandal
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students, NANS, has called for an immediate and transparent investigation into the alleged sexual misconduct involving a lecturer at Nnamdi Azikiwe University, UNIZIK, Awka, Anambra State. The students’ body made the demand in a statement issued by its National P

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Messi leads race for Golden Boot ahead of final [Top 7]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, is now leading the race for the Golden Boot ahead of Sunday’s World Cup final against Spain. Although Messi did not find the back of the net in the 2-1 win over England, he provided two match-winning assists. That brings his tally in the tournament to four

item.title: ‘Our schools must never become theatres of fear’ – Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Mr Peter Obi, has condemned the abduction of a school principal, students and a National Examinations Council, NECO, official during an examination in Kogi State, describing the incident as a disturbing reflection of the country’s worse

item.title: 2026 World Cup: I have no regrets – Tuchel insists as England lose to Argentina
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: England manager, Thomas Tuchel, has insisted he has “no regrets” about his tactical decision, as they threw a goal lead to lose to Argentina in the semi-final of the 2026 World Cup. Anthony Gordon had given the Three Lions the lead in the 55th minute of the match in Atlanta. But the curr

item.title: Fintiri Applauds Tinubu Over Dual Mandate Approval For FCE Yola
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Adamawa State Governor, Rt. Hon. Ahmadu Umaru Fintiri, has commended President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for approving dual mandate status for the Federal College of Education (FCE), Yola, describing the decision as a significant milestone for teacher education and higher learning in Nigeria. Governor Finti

item.title: Healthy meals for millions: CAPPA commends FG’s new food procurement policy
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: CAPPA added that it would continue working with relevant stakeholders to support implementation of the guidelines through research, public education, constructive engagement and independent monitoring.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: They deserve to be in final – Shearer reacts as Argentina beat England
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Newcastle United striker, Alan Shearer, has said Argentina deserved to beat England and qualify for the final of the 2026 World Cup. The Three Lions threw away a second-half lead to lose 2-1 to the defending champions on Tuesday night. Anthony Gordon ghosted in at the back post in t

item.title: 2026 World Cup: It was a gamble – Rooney names who to blame as England lose to Argentina
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Manchester United striker, Wayne Rooney, has criticized England manager, Thomas Tuchel after a 2-1 defeat to Argentina at the 2026 World Cup. The Three Lions were leading 1-0 in the semi-final clash, thanks to Anthony Gordon’s goal, until the last five minutes. The defending champions s

item.title: Kane rues England’s World Cup collapse after Argentina comeback
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: England captain Harry Kane admitted he was devastated after the Three Lions surrendered a second-half lead to lose 2-1 to defending champions Argentina in the semi-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, describing the defeat as another painful setback for a team that had come within touching distance of

item.title: Minister Satisfied With CBT Adoption As 1.3m Candidates Take NECO Exam
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Federal government has expressed satisfaction with the conduct of the ongoing National Examinations Council (NECO) school-based Senior School Certificate Examination (SSCE), as 1,378,048 candidates sit for the examination. The minister of State for Education, Prof Suwaiba Said Ahmad, gave the assess

item.title: Kwara APC Reconciliation Committee Begins Work, Extends Olive Branch To G15, Others
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The 5- member Kwara State All Progressives Congress Reconciliation Committee has commenced work in earnest. The committee led by a veteran politician and administrator , Colonel AbdulWahab Lawal(rtd) swung into action immediately after its inauguration on Wednesday, July 8, 2026. The committee membe

item.title: Troops arrest suspected foreign spy after foiled terrorist infiltration in Borno
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have arrested a suspected foreign spy during a follow-up operation in Abadam Local Government Area of Borno State, days after they foiled an attempted terrorist infiltration on a military position in the area. The development was disclosed in a post shared on X on Wedne

item.title: Insecurity: Etiki Federal university seeks police collobaration
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Vice-Chancellor of the Federal University of Technology and Environmental Sciences, Iyin-Ekiti, Professor James Olugbenga Aribisala, has sought stronger security collaboration with the Ekiti State Police Command. Professor Aribisala made the call on Tuesday when he led the management team

item.title: Court orders permanent forfeiture of 48 assets tied to Malami
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal High Court, Abuja, has ordered the permanent forfeiture of 48 properties associated with former Attorney General of the Federation and Minister of Justice, Abubakar Malami, to the Federal Government. The post Court orders permanent forfeiture of 48 assets tied to Malami appeared first on

item.title: Goodluck Jonathan: Nigeria’s most cowardly politician! By Olu Fasan
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Twice in recent presidential election cycles, Goodluck Jonathan, Nigeria’s former one-term president, allowed speculations to spread like wildfire that he would seek the presidency again. But twice, without as much as a whimper, the speculations fizzled out into thin air like a puff of wind. Recentl

item.title: 50 killed, scores displaced in one week in Plateau –Indigenous youths
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A coalition of indigenous youth groups in Plateau State has said that no fewer than 50 people have been killed and hundreds displaced in separate attacks across various parts of the state within the last one week. The post 50 killed, scores displaced in one week in Plateau –Indigenous youths appeare

item.title: Plateau killings: Ruling on Angwan/Rukuba jurisdiction for Aug ust 11
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Plateau State State High Court, Jos has fixed August 11 2026, to rule on an application challenging its jurisdiction to try four persons arraigned over their alleged involvement in the Palm Sunday Angwan Rukuba killings. The post Plateau killings: Ruling on Angwan/Rukuba jurisdiction for Aug ust 1

item.title: Shield Africa Empowers Benue Youths, Women in Fish Farming
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: To boost food security and tackle unemployment, the Shield Africa Sustainability and Development Initiative has graduated beneficiaries of the 2025/2026 cohort of its Farmers’ Academy (SAFA) in Benue State. The ceremony came after an intensive six-month free training on fish production and man

item.title: Court adjourns Blessing CEO’s N36m fraud trial to July 20
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The trial of social media influencer and relationship therapist, Okoro Blessing Nkiruka, popularly known as Blessing CEO, was yesterday adjourned until July 20 after she failed to appear before the Federal High Court, Ikoyi, Lagos. The post Court adjourns Blessing CEO’s N36m fraud trial to July 20

item.title: 2027: Christian Group Seeks Equal Political Space For Candidates
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Christian Rural and Urban Development Association of Nigeria (CRUDAN) has called on the federal government to ensure a credible and transparent electoral processes in the 2027 general elections. The association also called for inclusivity and equal political space for all candidates, adding that

item.title: Lagos police arrest suspect with firearms, Indian hemp
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: By Faheem Lawal and Ogunsolu Oluwalonimi Operatives of the Lagos State Police Command have arrested a 22-year-old suspect and recovered two firearms, live ammunition and substances suspected to be Indian hemp during a stop-and-search operation along Adetola Road, Aguda, even as a manhunt has been la

item.title: Tinubu Inaugurates 20 Judges’ Quarters In Abuja
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has reaffirmed his administration’s commitment to strengthening judicial independence through sustained investment in infrastructure, declaring that judges can only dispense justice effectively when they work and live in secure, dignified and conducive environments. Repre

item.title: Former Conduct Tribunal Chairman Gets N100m Bail
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A former chairman of the Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT), Mr Danladi Umar, has been granted bail by Justice Peter Kekemeke of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court sitting in Maitama, Abuja. He was granted bail in the sum of N100 million. The court, in a brief ruling, ordered that the defend

item.title: Names of 50,000 successful police constables released
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Police Service Commission (PSC) has released the names of successful applicants for the recruitment of 50,000 Constables into the Nigeria Police Force. The post Names of 50,000 successful police constables released appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: With his voice still shaking after Argentina’s stunning comeback against England, Albiceleste striker Lautaro Martinez described his side's journey as "incredible." The reigning World Cup champions will now look to defend their crown against Spain on Sunday night. It sets up a mouthwatering clash be

item.title: 20-year-old commits suicide after alleging rape by content creator
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A 20-year-old woman has died by suicide after allegedly being raped by a content creator who had promised to train her in content creation in Delta State. The post 20-year-old commits suicide after alleging rape by content creator appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Nigerians Want Justice, Not Religious Ticket In 2027 – Adebayo
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Presidential candidate of the Social Democratic Party (SDP), Prince Adewole Adebayo, has said Nigerians have rejected religious politics and are demanding justice and competence ahead of the 2027 elections. Speaking on Arise Television’s Morning Show, Adebayo stressed that the President Bola Tinubu

item.description: The Commissioner of Police, Delta State Command, CP Yemi Oyeniyi, has directed patrol and guard officers in all police divisions across the state, as well as leaders of vigilante groups, forest guards and other recognised civil security outfits, to intensify intelligence gathering and sustain patrol

item.title: Natasha Asks NAFDAC To Develop Indigenous Herbal Pharmaceutical Industry
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Senator Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan, representing Kogi Central Senatorial District, has appealed to the National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) to spearhead the development of a robust pharmaceutical industry built on Nigeria’s indigenous medicinal plants. She said the init

item.title: Online publisher remanded over defamatory publications against Soludo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: An online publisher, Ejike Ofoegbu, publisher of uIgbo Times Magazine and INews, was on Monday arraigned before a Magistrate Court sitting in Amawbia, Awka South Local Government Area of Anambra State, over alleged defamatory publications against Governor Prof. Chukwuma Soludo. The claims against Investigative Directorate Against Corruption head Andrea Johnson are the final nail in the coffin of his promise. He has failed, partially because of his own action

item.title: Zimbabwe: Police Arrest More Than 2000 in Nationwide Crime and Traffic Crackdown
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [263Chat] More than 2 000 people have been arrested since the Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) launched a nationwide operation targeting crime and traffic offences, authorities have said.

item.title: Tanzania: Tanzania Orders Immediate Closure of a Factory Producing Banned Plastic Bags
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Daily News] Dar es Salaam -- THE Tanzanian government has ordered the immediate closure of an illegal plastic bag manufacturing factory in Chanika, Dar es Salaam, after it was found producing banned plastic carrier bags in violation of the country's environmental laws.

item.title: High blood pressure driving stroke surge among young Nigerians, study finds
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Stroke is no longer just an older person’s disease. Experts in a new study revealed that high blood pressure is the leading cause of stroke among young adults in Northern Nigeria, raising fresh concerns about a silent health crisis affecting people in their most productive years. The post High blood

item.dc:creator: PM News

item.title: IEA Sees $6.5 Trillion At Risk if China Imposes Rare-Earth Curbs
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s rare earth export curbs could put $6.5 trillion of downstream production outside the country at risk each year if fully implemented, the International Energy Agency warned in a report on rare earths and critical minerals.

item.title: ‘For 150 days, Nasir El-Rufai has been detained. Nigeria’s partners must speak up’
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: The continued imprisonment of outspoken politician and Tinubu critic shines a spotlight on ‘lawfare’ in Nigeria, writes El-Rufai’s wife.

item.description: Latest government measure aimed at curbing digital harm comes after under-16 social media ban announced last month.

item.title: Nickel Hits Three-Week High on Indonesia Supply Risks, Fed View
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Nickel climbed to a three-week high as expectations for Federal Reserve rate hikes faded, while uncertainty over mining policy in top producer Indonesia clouded the supply outlook.

item.description: [Nyasa Times] More than 100 girls under the age of 18 are believed to be involved in sex work in Rumphi, according to warnings issued by campaigners at a stakeholders' meeting this week.

item.title: Zimbabwe: Zimbabwean Migrants in Louis Trichardt Fear Losing Access to Health Services
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Health-e] Zimbabwean immigrants living in Louis Trichardt say they are being turned away from clinics or told their latest chronic medication collection will be their last, raising fears that anti-immigrant pressure is starting to affect access to healthcare.

item.title: Sudan: Looted Gold and Gum Arabic Are Bankrolling Sudan's War, UN Warns
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [UN News] Alongside gold, the little-known Sudanese commodity gum arabic - used in soft drinks, food, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals - continues to fuel the war.

item.title: If you want to create a button from scratch, you must first create the universe
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://madcampos.dev/blog/2026/07/accessibility-from-scratch/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48930136 Points: 42 # Comments: 5

item.title: WORLD IN BRIEF: France legalises assisted dying, Africa’s first white president dies at 82, SpaceX falls below IPO price and other stories
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Iran threatens wider trade disruption as US intensifies military strikes Iran has threatened to block additional regional trade routes as read more WORLD IN BRIEF: France legalises assisted dying, Africa’s first white president dies at 82, SpaceX falls below IPO price and other stories

item.title: Syngenta’s $5 Billion HK IPO Is Said to Face Fresh Delays
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Syngenta Group’s planned $5 billion Hong Kong initial public offering is facing delays as the seed and pesticide company waits for better conditions in the agriculture sector, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Senior Chinese delegation visits North Korea for talks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: China's fourth-highest-ranked official, Wang Huning, held talks with a top official of North Korea's governing party.

item.title: Financial reporting and genetically modified organism disclosures
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: The International Sustainability Standard Board (ISSB) is currently enhancing Sustainability Accounting Standards Board (SASB) priority The post Financial reporting and genetically modified organism disclosures appeared first on The Guardian Nigeria News - Nigeria and World News.

item.title: Asian Gas Prices Rise to Highest Since March on Mideast Tensions
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Asian liquefied natural gas prices rose to the highest since late March as fresh hostilities in the Middle East deepened concerns that shipping through the key Strait of Hormuz will remain disrupted for longer.

item.title: CIPM holds virtual induction, admits 78 HR professionals
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Chartered Institute of Personnel Management of Nigeria (CIPM) has admitted 78 new members into the human resource profession in its first-ever virtual induction ceremony. The landmark 71st Induction Ceremony underscored the Institute’s commitment to innovation, professional excellence and digita

item.title: FG sets N3.6bn to support tailors under SUPA programme
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Federal Government has stated that it has set aside N3.6bn to support tailors under the Industrial Training Fund’s Skill Up Artisans Programme (SUPA), as part of efforts to strengthen Nigeria’s informal sector and boost job creation. The Director-General and Chief Executive of the Industrial Tra

item.title: 50 firms eye investment in Yobe
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Yobe State Government has said more than 50 local and foreign investors have expressed interest in investing in key sectors of the state’s economy, with officials projecting a major investment breakthrough before the end of the year. The Director-General of the Yobe State Agency for Public-Priva

item.title: FMBN introduces diaspora mortgage loan for Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN) has announced that it has introduced the Diaspora Mortgage Loan, aimed at enabling Nigerians living abroad who contribute to the National Housing Fund Scheme to access affordable mortgages for homeownership and property in the country. Speaking at the 20th

item.title: South Korea’s international adoptees seek justice, not homecoming
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: South Korea's government has acknowledged wrongs, but advocates say accountability is still lacking.
